title: Create full-screen canvas shell
summary: Wire the existing canvas shell modules (scene.ts, resize.ts, renderLoop.ts) into main.ts so the full-screen dark canvas is rendered on load, resizes with the viewport, and produces no scrollbars. Most infrastructure already exists; the work is integration and verification.

existingFiles.src/visual/scene.ts: Creates a 2D canvas, sizes to window, fills black, appends to container. Returns { canvas, ctx }.
existingFiles.src/visual/resize.ts: attachResizeHandler(canvas, ctx) listens to window resize, updates canvas dimensions, returns cleanup fn.
existingFiles.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: startLoop(canvas, ctx) runs requestAnimationFrame loop, clears canvas to black each frame.
existingFiles.src/style.css: Already has html/body overflow:hidden, margin:0, 100vw/100vh, #app position:fixed, canvas display:block, dark background.
existingFiles.src/main.ts: Fetches geo, reads signals, inits seed, inits audio, creates mute button. Does NOT call initScene, attachResizeHandler, or startLoop.

currentState.tests: tests/canvas-shell.test.ts has ~16 tests covering initScene, resize, CSS, dark background, render loop, privacy, and main.ts integration.
currentState.gap: main.ts does not import or call the visual modules. The canvas never appears at runtime.

steps.action: Update src/main.ts to import and call initScene, attachResizeHandler, and startLoop
steps.details: After existing startup logic, get the #app container, call initScene(container), call attachResizeHandler(canvas, ctx), call startLoop(canvas, ctx). Import style.css if not already imported. The canvas must be the first visual element added to #app so UI overlays layer on top.
steps.reason: This is the only missing wiring — the modules exist but are never invoked.

steps.details: Ensure `import './style.css'` is present at the top of main.ts so Vite processes the CSS.
steps.reason: CSS rules for overflow:hidden, margin:0, and dark background must be active for acceptance criteria.

steps.details: The test suite already covers all four acceptance criteria: canvas fills viewport, resize updates render size, no scrollbars (overflow:hidden), dark background. Confirm all pass after the integration change.
steps.reason: Tests were written ahead of integration; passing them confirms the ticket is done.

steps.details: Verify no TypeScript or bundling errors.
steps.reason: Definition of Done requires build passes.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Canvas fills the viewport: initScene sets canvas.width = window.innerWidth, canvas.height = window.innerHeight. CSS sets html/body to 100vw/100vh.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Viewport resize updates render size: attachResizeHandler listens to window resize and updates canvas dimensions.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.No scrollbars appear: CSS sets overflow:hidden on html and body, margin:0, padding:0.
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Canvas is shown on a dark background: initScene fills canvas with #000000. renderLoop clears to black each frame. body background-color is #000.

risksAndNotes: Three.js is not installed. The current approach uses 2D Canvas API, which satisfies this ticket. Three.js can be introduced in a later story when actual 3D visuals are needed.
The render loop currently only clears to black — this is intentional per the ticket note: 'Rendering backend can still be a placeholder.'
Canvas initialization should happen early (before audio init) so the user sees the dark canvas immediately even if audio autoplay is blocked.
estimatedComplexity: trivial — ~10 lines of integration code in main.ts, all supporting modules already exist and are tested
